title: Object 66
wikitext: {{VoidedTheme}} {{DISPLAYTITLE: Object 66: Abyssal Soup}} {{CSS|content= @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Playwrite+DE+Grund:wght@100..400&family=Tagesschrift&display=swap'); @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Coral+Pixels&display=swap'); /* Font */ html .resizable-container, .page-content h1, .page-content h2, .page-content h3, .page-content h4, .page-content h5, .page-content h6 { font-family: 'Tagesschrift'}} __NOTOC__ ==Description== {{Blurimage|file=Abyssal_Soup.png|caption=An image of Abyssal Soup.}} '''Abyssal Soup''' The chemical name for '''Abyssal Soup''' is Mg(CHO₂)₃SnI, or Magnesium tris(formato)tin(II) iodide. , sometimes referred to as '''Niandra's First Choice''', is a runny, odorless, pitch black liquid which can be obtained from various levels. '''Abyssal Soup''' has several properties depending on the person drinking it. Each individual will have a different experience when consuming '''Abyssal Soup''', and effects are dependent on personality. '''Object 66''' has a melting point of -103°C and a boiling point of 147°C. Its taste is similar to that of chicken noodle soup. ==Obtainment and Discovery== '''Abyssal Soup''' is typically found within the walls of [[The Hub]], where it is described as "pressing on it until it gives" by [[M.E.G.]] agent Niandra Drews of Team D-3. The nickname '''Niandra's First Choice''' was given as she was the agent who made the official discovery of this substance and is a frequent user, opting to carry it around in lieu of [[Object 1|Almond Water]]. Niandra Drews refuses to comment about how she made the discovery of '''Abyssal Soup''' in the first place. Obtaining '''Abyssal Soup''' is an arduous task, and should only be carried out if the effects are determined to be worth the effort by the user. The collection is split into two parts: extraction and purification. First, wanderers must use a large, flat board such as a wooden plank or a metal sheet and push it up firmly against the wall of [[The Hub]]. Next, a large container must be placed below for collection. The board must then be forcefully pressed into the wall until a black, watery liquid begins to trickle down into the collection container. Typically, around four liters of raw '''Abyssal Soup''' can be collected within an hour. Once a suitable amount of raw '''Abyssal Soup''' is collected, wanderers must then heat it over an open fire. Ensure a metal container is used for the best results. Wanderers must ensure the liquid does not boil, and should remove the '''Abyssal Soup''' from over the fire if it begins to do so. After approximately 30 minutes of this task, wanderers must sieve through the liquid and remove any large chunks or particles that may still remain. Lastly, the remaining filtrate can be stored for future use or can be drunk outright. ==Different Effects== {{Blurimage|file=Personality.png|caption=A guide on the different types of personalities.}} It is imperative that one understands their own personality type before consuming '''Abyssal Soup'''. Its effects vary greatly between the five types of personalities indicated, and effects may be lethal. It is advised to only drink '''Abyssal Soup''' at one's own discretion. {| class="article-table" style="border: 2px solid #FFFFFF; margin:0;" |+ |Personality Type |style="border-left:solid 2px #FFFFFF" | Effects |- |Type A |style="border-left:solid 2px #FFFFFF" |Increases constitution, strength and reaction time for four hours. |- |Type B |style="border-left:solid 2px #FFFFFF" |Increases problem-solving capability, decreases spatial awareness for three hours. Causes drowsiness afterwards. |- |Type C |style="border-left:solid 2px #FFFFFF" |Decreases reaction time, increases endurance for one hour. |- |Type D |style="border-left:solid 2px #FFFFFF" |Inflicts an intense feeling of being watched and increases paranoia levels for five hours, replenishes energy levels. |- |Type X |style="border-left:solid 2px #FFFFFF" |Combination of the two personality effects. |- |} ==Reactions== '''Abyssal Soup''' is noted to react with certain other commonly found objects. Important reactions will be listed below. ===Almond WaterMg(CHO₂)₃SnI + H₂O → Mg²⁺(aq) + 3 HCOO⁻(aq) + SnI⁺(aq) → [Sn(OH)I + HI]=== Formate decomposes slightly into CO₂ (weak effervescence). Tin(II) iodide hydroxide (Sn(OH)I) forms a milky suspension. Solution turns mildly acidic (HI byproduct) and begins to give off a violet glow. No known use but could be utilized in glow sticks. ===EthanolMg(CHO₂)₃SnI + C₂H₅OH → Mg(CHO₂)₂ + CH₃CHO + SnI₂ + HCOOH=== Solution turns yellow due to formation of SnI₂. Acetaldehyde (nail polish remover scent) gives off a pungent odour. Warm to the touch as it is an exothermic reaction. The mixture produces gases which may induce hallucinations if inhaled, possibly due to a tin-ethanol neurotoxicity. Used as a chemical smoke grenade to subdue masses of people or entities. ===Liquid PainMg(CHO₂)₃SnI + RnBr₆ → [MgBr₂ + SnBr₄ + 3 CO₂ + Rn] + I₂ + Screaming=== CO₂ and radioactive radon (Rn) gas is formed. Iodine (I₂) sublimation produces purple gases. Produces an immense amount of heat, able to melt plastic containers. Reaction of magnesium and bromine produces high frequency "screaming" loud enough to damage eardrums. SnBr₄ polymers expand rapidly and expel outwards violently. Typically used in radioactive grenades where both reactants mix after a pin is pulled and the grenade is thrown. ==Final Notes== The reaction of '''Abyssal Soup''' and [[Object 12|Liquid Pain]] or ethanol is extremely discouraged, due to the numerous hazards it poses. the walls bleed for you. {{Footnotes}} ---- {{Collapse|num=3|initial='''> Open Author Information'''|final='''> Close Author Information'''|body= Page written by [[User:JustASpecificGuy|JustASpecificGuy]].}} [[Category:Objects]]
